summary.text: [0-2] A static title card features white, scratchy serif text reading "Apple Butter made with dehydrated apples" centered on a dark maroon background. [2-8] The video cuts to a still life arrangement on a patterned cloth against a white wall. A large glass jar filled with dried apple slices sits beside a smaller jar containing a dark brown spread. Behind them stands a ceramic rooster figurine decorated with patriotic stars and stripes. Vertical red text runs along the left edge. [8-14] The scene changes to a white ceramic slow cooker on a countertop. Inside the pot, a quantity of dried apple slices rests at the bottom. In the background, a yellow spray can and other kitchen items are visible. The camera remains fixed on the appliance.

[14-16] A white ceramic slow cooker sits on a countertop containing tan dried apple slices, with a yellow spray can and glass jars visible in the background. [16-20] A person's hand reaches into the basin to stir and lift a large clump of the wrinkled fruit pieces. [20-24] Two hands then work to break apart the clump inside the pot before withdrawing, leaving the slices scattered. [24-28] Finally, a clear plastic bag filled with more dried apple slices is brought over the appliance, and the contents are dumped in to add to the existing layer.

[28-44] A pair of hands transfers tan, dried apple slices from a clear plastic bag into a white ceramic slow cooker. Initially, the person shakes and squeezes the crumpled bag to empty remaining fruit onto an existing pile at the basin's bottom. Subsequently, the hands hold a large, dense clump of dried apple slices above the pot. The person manually pulls apart the mass, separating individual wrinkled pieces and dropping them into the slow cooker until the clump is fully broken down and added to the layer of dehydrated fruit inside.

[44-48] A white ceramic slow cooker basin holds several large clumps of tan dried apple slices resting at the bottom. Background items include a yellow spray can and glass jars. A silver metallic object partially enters the top right corner. [48-56] A silver metallic kettle positions above the basin and pours a continuous stream of clear water directly onto the tan dried apple slices. The liquid flows steadily, moistening and saturating the dehydrated fruit pieces as they hydrate. The text "CROCK-POT" is visible on the front of the white appliance. [56-58] The silver metallic kettle stops dispensing liquid as the clear stream thins and disappears completely. The tan dried apple slices remain resting at the bottom, appearing hydrated from the pouring action, while the kettle spout stays positioned over the pot.

[58-60] A silver metallic kettle remains stationary above a white ceramic slow cooker after ceasing flow, with tan dried apple slices sitting hydrated in liquid at the bottom. [60-64] A thin, clear stream resumes pouring from the kettle onto the tan dried apple slices, steadily saturating the fruit pile within the white basin as the "CROCK-POT" logo is visible on the appliance's exterior and a yellow spray can sits in the background. [64-68] Steam rises as the water level increases while the clear stream continues hitting the tan dried apple slices, further hydrating the fruit. [68-70] The stream shifts to fall directly onto the tan dried apple slices on the right side of the pot. [70-72] The silver kettle ceases pouring, leaving the fruit in liquid, and a hand with visible fingers enters the frame on the right near the pot's rim.

[72-74] A white ceramic slow cooker remains stationary on the countertop, filled with tan dried apple slices submerged in liquid. No water stream is visible pouring from above, and the hand seen previously has withdrawn from the frame. The hydrated fruit pieces sit undisturbed at the bottom of the basin. [74-80] The scene cuts to a close-up of the white basin filled with plump, light tan cooked apple pieces. A silver metal fork rests among the soft, rehydrated fruit slices. The apples appear fully hydrated and tender, with the arrangement and lighting conditions remaining constant without any visible motion. [80-82] A blurry finger or hand partially appears on the far left edge, hovering near the rim while the food and utensil remain undisturbed. [82-86] The hand reaches in from the left, lifting a single piece of the cooked, tan apple slice vertically into the air. The silver fork remains stationary amidst the pile of soft, rehydrated fruit. The suspended slice showcases a tender texture against the static background of kitchen items.

[86-90] A hand holds a cooked, tan apple slice vertically above a white ceramic basin filled with soft, rehydrated fruit. A silver fork rests stationary in the pile. The hand releases the slice back into the basin and withdraws from the frame. [90-100] The scene remains static, showing the white ceramic slow cooker filled with cooked apple slices. The silver fork lies undisturbed among the fruit. No hands are visible. Blurry kitchen jars appear in the background. The lighting and angle stay constant, displaying the prepared food without new action.

[100-108] A white ceramic slow cooker is filled with cooked tan apple slices, with a silver fork resting undisturbed among the soft, rehydrated fruit pieces. The camera cuts to a wider shot revealing the appliance's exterior on a counter, displaying white side handles, a control dial with settings, and "CROCK-POT" text. Blurry kitchen jars and items persist in the background behind the pot. [108-114] A hand enters the frame holding a silver utensil and actively stirs the cooked tan apple slices inside the white slow cooker. The mixing motion shifts the soft fruit pieces around within the ceramic basin. The appliance stays stationary on the counter throughout the stirring action, with a yellow can visible in the background.

[114-118] A hand lifts and stirs soft, tan apple slices inside a white ceramic slow cooker with a silver utensil, turning the cooked fruit pieces within the basin. [118-120] A white handheld immersion blender is inserted vertically into the center of the apples, the silver shaft entering the fruit mixture. [120-128] The white immersion blender remains vertically submerged in the tan apple chunks. A hand holds the white motor housing steady above the rim while the silver shaft stays buried in the fruit. The appliance sits stationary on the counter with a yellow can visible in the background.

[128-130] A white immersion blender with a silver shaft remains vertically inserted into tan apple chunks inside a white slow cooker. The white motor housing is held steady above the rim while the appliance sits on the counter with a yellow spray can visible in the background. [130-142] The scene cuts to the white slow cooker containing a smooth, dark brown puree instead of chunky apples. A wooden spoon actively stirs the thick, uniform mixture within the ceramic basin, indicating the blending is complete. The texture appears creamy and consistent throughout the pot. A hand becomes visible on the right edge of the frame, resting on the counter near the appliance. The spoon lifts and folds the creamy mixture, showcasing its consistency as it drips back into the pot while the hand holding it is partially visible.

[142-146] A person wearing a teal shirt holds a small white plastic container near a white slow cooker filled with smooth brown puree, where a wooden spoon rests undisturbed. [146-152] The hand tilts the white container to pour a white powdery substance into the brown mixture, then lifts the empty vessel away from the appliance. [152-156] Next, a hand brings a dark blue measuring cup filled with white granules over the pot and pours the contents, forming a large white pile on top of the brown puree next to the stationary wooden spoon.

[156-158] A hand pours white granules from a dark blue measuring cup onto brown puree in a white slow cooker, forming a large mound. [158-172] A hand grasps a wooden spoon to stir the mixture, pushing into the white granules and folding them into the brown puree. The white mound breaks apart and blends with the underlying substance. As stirring continues, the granules are fully incorporated, resulting in a uniform, smooth, dense brown consistency within the ceramic pot.

[172-186] A wooden spoon actively stirs a thick, tan-brown puree inside a white ceramic slow cooker. The mixture maintains a smooth, uniform, and dense consistency as the spoon lifts, folds, and turns the substance. A hand is visible holding the utensil, moving it in a steady motion to ensure the ingredients remain thoroughly blended within the pot. The texture appears creamy and fully incorporated throughout the continuous mixing action. The spoon vigorously mixes the viscous liquid without pausing, keeping the contents uniform.

[186-200] A hand uses a wooden spoon to vigorously stir a thick, glossy brown puree inside a white ceramic slow cooker. The spoon lifts and folds the dense, tan-brown mixture, which clings heavily to the utensil, demonstrating its viscous and creamy consistency. The stirring motion is steady and continuous, ensuring the substance remains smooth and uniform throughout the pot. This action prevents the thick puree from sticking to the interior surfaces of the slow cooker basin. The texture appears heavy and smooth as it is turned over repeatedly within the appliance.

A hand maneuvers a wooden spoon through a thick, glossy dark brown puree inside a white ceramic slow cooker. The viscous mixture clings heavily to the utensil as it is lifted and turned, showcasing a dense, smooth consistency. The stirring motion is steady, folding the substance back into the pot to ensure uniformity and prevent the mixture from adhering to the ceramic sides. Towards the end, the stirring pauses briefly as the spoon lifts a heavy coat of the puree, highlighting the substantial texture of the cooked substance within the basin.

[216-226] A hand maneuvers a wooden spoon through a thick, glossy dark brown puree inside a white ceramic slow cooker. The spoon stirs the viscous mixture, folding it to ensure a smooth, uniform consistency while preventing it from sticking to the pot's interior. The dense substance clings heavily to the utensil as it is lifted and turned, showcasing its high viscosity. At times, the spoon lifts a heavy dollop of the puree aloft before lowering back into the pot to resume mixing the smooth, dark contents thoroughly.

[226-238] A hand maneuvers a wooden spoon through a thick, dark brown puree inside a white ceramic slow cooker. The spoon lifts a heavy, glossy dollop of the viscous mixture, highlighting its dense consistency. The hand lowers the spoon back into the cooker, resuming stirring to fold the substance and ensure a smooth texture. Repeatedly, the spoon lifts heavy portions of the dark brown apple butter, displaying its viscosity. The hand continues to stir the dense mixture, folding the glossy liquid to prevent sticking to the sides of the white ceramic vessel and maintaining uniform consistency.

[214-248] A hand uses a wooden spoon to stir a thick, dark brown puree inside a white ceramic slow cooker. The viscous, glossy mixture clings heavily to the utensil as it is repeatedly lifted and lowered to ensure a smooth consistency and prevent sticking. The spoon churns the heavy liquid, creating swirls and ripples on the surface while agitating the substance within the pot. [248-250] The view changes to a ceramic rooster figurine decorated with patriotic stars and stripes, featuring a red comb and yellow beak on a textured brown base. A patterned cloth lies in the foreground, and vertical red text reading "STONY RUNNERS" appears on the left against a white wall.
